name: Tennie C. Haddox
description: A prospective young lady, who will someday be a "school marm," a vocation she terms a "general art." A girl with high marks "sparkling" in Political Economy and Domestic Science. Class treasurer—she sure can keep money.

name: Annie Rowan
description: She had better get some blocks to stand on around like a large lady.

name: Florence Stout
description: A quiet, well-behaved student. An example for all who came in contact with her. Will make a kind teacher.

name: Hattie Ray
description: What she is today she is always. Has many friends and is loved by all.

name: Gladys Jones
description: Oh, how she does typewrite. Very popular and loved by all. Is sure to make good at her work.

name: Mary Baxter Smith
description: The most modest of the modest and not unlike her namesake, "The Virgin Mary." Good in Psychology and Chemistry.

name: Mary Bertha Whaley
description: A quiet and hard-working student. Sweet lil' lady with pretty eyes. Many friends.

name: Ethel C. Grinton
description: An art student with a promising future. Noted for her modesty. Ethel is a fine girl.

name: Hawkins, Virginia
description: "An all-round" student who is expected to pursue a college course and will make good.

name: Titus, J. K.
description: An athletic youth who always proves himself a star in the pinches. In both baseball and football Titus shows himself to be an athlete. A youth of integrity, who will be a successful business man.

name: Maddox, Cassie Emily
description: An all-round student—especially good in Latin. One of those genial, hand-lending girls whose kind face alone volunteers all the aid its owner can give. Something makes her say she is futureless—Oh! modesty fly away.

name: Minor, Charles P.
description: Minor has proven himself to be one of the great young men of the school. He won the medal in the Oratorical Contest in 1914. Minor is energetic and earnest, but does not object to a little fun now and then. Minor will study dentistry after leaving Normal.

name: Garner, Anna M.
description: A sister to the other one, and just as fine. We have had few girls who have equaled her in "general sweetness" of disposition.

name: Garrett, Bernie Lee
description: "General Course" has been her watchword, and a "general" over that "course" has Bernice Lee been. A girl who minds her own business, mixing good sense with it.

name: Lanier, L. B.
description: One of our special students and a minister's wife. She loves "Old Normal" and will always be with us.

name: Mitchel, Scott
description: An exceptionally good student, has great executive ability.

name: Broaddus, Lavinia D.
description: Class model (hee! hee!), very refined and cultured and makes a hit with the Dean by praying in Wednesday evening prayer service. "She can go."

name: Coffield, Miley Ray
description: Coffield is a serious, well behaved fellow who lost kiddishness and adopted manliness at an early age. He is a giant in Math. and Science. Will go to college, and is one of the members of the Trinity.

name: Gordon, Mai Agnes
description: We call her. and she can play some music. Gets in "wrong" occasionally just to break the monotony.

name: Nichols, Claude E.
description: The most popular young man on "Normal Hill," and a very thoughtful student. Claude is an all-round athlete and is Captain of the baseball team. Nichols is also an orator; he won the medal in the Oratorical Contest in 1915.

name: Garner, Ada M.
description: One of the "C. S. H. S." girls who has spent two years with us and has made a "rep" for her town school—all love her.

name: Covington, Virginia Marie
description: Proud of being a farmer's daughter; noted for her excellent alto voice. A member of the Glee Club, Mozart, and double quartette.

name: Wilson, Gertrude
motto: "Be still sad heart and cease your pining."
description: One of the class "dolls," and chief "noise" maker. Very jolly and attractive. Her motto: Around here in crocheting and needle work.

name: Ridgway, James F.
description: Some singer he is. Takes to music and is an adept too with a cornet. Taken all in all, Ridgway is one of the finest young men that ever entered this school. A most obliging and good-natured fellow. Everybody likes Ridgway.

name: Hammonds, Roberta V.
description: A quiet girl (?) and as you have observed. Promising with the motto, whatever's worth doing at all is worth doing well. Good in sewing.

name: Vanleer, Archie
description: A conscientious young man whose exceptional conduct might well serve as a model for all who in their turn shall follow him. He is a business young man and one who is sure to make good in life where honesty and industry are the essentials of success.

name: Alexander, Roberta E.
description: Most popular of the Senior Class. She won a medal in the essay contest—an all-round student.

name: Black, William
description: Black is one of those young men whom you hear little. He does not waste his energy in making a noise, but uses it in filling his grey matter with knowledge. He always dresses neatly and is one of the most dignified young men of the Institution. Even talks to girls occasionally.

name: Boyd, Eunice
description: A very accomplished domestic young lady. Will make good at her work because of her positiveness.

name: Jones, James U.
description: A young man much liked and widely known. Humorous and original. A Jacksonian enthusiast. A sociable and clever youth. Has buried a deep love spot in the hearts of all his friends. He is one of the students of whom the institution is proud. Sparkling with brilliance.

name: Kelley, Lena
motto: "the only way to have a friend is to be one."
description: Very prominent young lady—especially loved by the boys (?), (I wonder why?) Nevertheless.

name: Turner, H. O.
description: A hard working student and is noted for his moral courage. Homer makes a success at everything he undertakes. He has won honor on the football field and is well liked by the student body.

name: Berryman, Katie
description: A thin, grave, pious-looking young lady with a sense of humor and a good head with plenty of grey matter in it. She actually made that good looking dress skirt which she wore Easter Sunday. There with the needle.

name: Fielding, Anderson
description: Fielding is an athlete, a social star and a Christian worker. He is one of the best bass singers in the South, and we are proud of him.

name: Springfield, Valeria Gladys
description: A happy girl. She delights in singing and playing for others. She is industrious, zealous and unselfish.

name: Brown, Lander M.
description: Brown is very popular with the young ladies, and is noted for his shrewdness. He is a good student and will make a successful business man.

name: Poston, Roberta Beatrice
description: Hails from "Hoptown." She is a born poetess. A charming girl with many friends and noted for her smiles.

name: Christy, William
description: Christy has held various offices in the literary society including that of President. He is an enthusiastic athlete, and an earnest worker at whatever he sets his hands. He has a copious knowledge of Roberts Rules of Order.

name: Stewart, Audley
description: Audley is our little "Florida Girl." She "flies off the handle" occasionally, but it does not take her long to get right. All in all, she is a lovable girl.

name: Haynes, Clarence
description: Captain of Company B. A shark at Science and there with Latin. Knows what he comes to school for. Fine type of manhood. He is persevering and tenacious, and is one of those young men with high ideals and independence. Will pursue medical studies.
